title: Daily Briefing — Apr 06
headline: Extreme fear, volatility cooling, and energy rips while tech wobbles at the 20 MA.
body: Right, so markets are still frozen in extreme fear — Fear & Greed at 19.3 is textbook panic, but VIX has started to cool off (23.87, below the 20-day SMA). This is the beginning of a sentiment flip zone, not a spot to panic sell — remember, less trades = more wins when the herd runs for the exits.

Sector rotation signals are a bit messy: Energy (XLE) is still strong after an insane move (CL=F up 11.4%, USO +11.2%), but leadership is weakening. Tech (XLK, QQQ, NVDA) remains heavy — most are hovering on or just below the 20 MA (NVDA $177.39 vs 20 MA $177.62, SPY $655.83 vs 20 MA $658.09), while defensives like Staples (XLP), Healthcare (XLV), and Utilities (XLU) are showing relative resilience or signs of triggering. We've also got Financials (XLF) improving, with price perched above all key MAs.

Contrarian lens: Retail is still spooked out and margin calls may be pressuring the tape, but with VIX coming down and price near major MAs, this has all the ingredients of a mean reversion setup. Don't chase breakdowns here — abundance mindset, wait for confirmation. Remember, if it's in the press, it's already in the price.
callout_body:
SPY: Watching $658.09 (SMA20); below = stay defensive
NVDA: $177.62 SMA20 pivot
XLF: $49.14 SMA20 and holding all MAs, canary not dead
XLK: $136.18 SMA20 acting as resistance
CL=F: $111.54 (+11.4%) — parabolic risk
Fear & Greed: 19.3 = Extreme Fear, look for capitulation reversal
VIX: 23.87, fading from panic zone
action_items: If SPY/QQQ/NVDA close below 20 MA again, stay patient — no new longs until reclaim.
Watch XLF, XLU, and XLE for follow-through; close under 20 MAs would be your early risk-off signal.
closing_quote: If everyone's scared, that's shopping season — but only with confirmation. Follow the flows.
